critic_raw:
CRITIQUE — real_world_framing_007 (Multi-round, Run 2)

1. BEHAVIORAL FEEDBACK LOOP FROM ALERTS: Survey data confirms frequent mode switching. Passenger load changes at alerted stops affect delay dynamics. Model trained on pre-alert data. Deployment creates prediction-outcome coupling absent from training environment.

2. THRESHOLD NOT OPTIMIZED FOR PASSENGER TRUST: 0.60 threshold set by intuition. False alert costs (unnecessary ride-hail expense, trust erosion) not analyzed in threshold selection.
defender_raw:
DEFENSE — real_world_framing_007 (Multi-round, Run 2 — receives Critic output)

1. FEEDBACK LOOP: Survey data makes the behavioral response documented, not hypothetical. The magnitude of load effect and calibration drift is empirically open. Mark as EMPIRICALLY OPEN.

2. THRESHOLD: CONCEDE — intuitive threshold selection is inadequate for a passenger-facing system with documented behavioral consequences.

debate_rounds.critic_move: PROPOSE EMPIRICAL TEST: A/B test across randomly assigned stop clusters (30 stops alert-enabled, 30 stops no-alert control) at the proposed threshold for 60 days. Measure: ridership change at alerted vs. control stops, delay realization rates (did alerted delays actually occur?), and model calibration over time in alert vs. no-alert conditions. Critic wins if alerted stops show >5% ridership decline that correlates with delay pattern changes. Defense wins if ridership change is <3% and calibration metrics are stable.
debate_rounds.defender_move: ACCEPT EMPIRICAL TEST. The test is well-specified and directly measures both the behavioral response magnitude and calibration consequences. I accept the success/failure criteria.

adjudication_raw: All issues resolved after one round. Empirical test agreed for both issues. Verdict: empirical_test_agreed.

empirical_test.condition: A/B test: 30 stops alert-enabled vs. 30 control stops for 60 days at threshold variants.
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Alerted stops show >5% ridership decline correlating with delay pattern changes.
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Moderate decline concentrated on subset of routes.
